Title: Vanuxem, Jacques
Overview: Vanuxem was the first to investigate the history of the often-repeated theory that the kinds and queens on early gothic portals represent the rulers of France. His essay, Theories of Mabillon and Montfaucon, 1957, was a milestone in the study of the meaning of the statue-column (Branner).
Sources: "Alan Priest." Chartres Cathedral: Norton Critical Studies in Art History.New York: W. W. Norton, 1969, p. 168.
